name: sql-metadata

metadata.name: sql_metadata
metadata.summary: Uses tokenized query returned by python-sqlparse and generates query metadata

documentation: # sql-metadata [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/sql_metadata.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/sql_metadata) [![Tests](https://github.com/macbre/sql-metadata/actions/workflows/python-ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/macbre/sql-metadata/actions/workflows/python-ci.yml) [![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/macbre/sql-metadata/badge.svg?branch=master&1)](https://coveralls.io/github/macbre/sql-metadata?branch=master) [![Maintenance](https://img.shields.io/badge/maintained%3F-yes-green.svg)](https://github.com/macbre/sql-metadata/graphs/commit-activity) [![Downloads](https://pepy.tech/badge/sql-metadata/month)](https://pepy.tech/project/sql-metadata) Uses tokenized query returned by [`python-sqlparse`](https://github.com/andialbrecht/sqlparse) and generates query metadata. **Extracts column names and tables** used by the query. Automatically conduct **column alias resolution**, **sub queries aliases resolution** as well as **tables aliases resolving**. Provides also a helper for **normalization of SQL queries**. Supported queries syntax: * MySQL * PostgreSQL * Sqlite * MSSQL * [Apache Hive](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/Hive/LanguageManual+DML) (note that listed backends can differ quite substantially but should work in regard of query types supported by `sql-metadata`) You can test the capabilities of `sql-metadata` with an interactive demo: [https://sql-app.infocruncher.com/](https://sql-app.infocruncher.com/) ## Usage ``` pip install sql-metadata ``` ### Extracting raw sql-metadata tokens ```python from sql_metadata import Parser # extract raw sql-metadata tokens Parser("SELECT * FROM foo").tokens # ['SELECT', '*', 'FROM', 'foo'] ``` ### Extracting columns from query ```python from sql_metadata import Parser # get columns from query - for more examples see `tests/test_getting_columns.py` Parser("SELECT test, id FROM foo, bar").columns # ['test', 'id'] Parser("INSERT /* VoteHelper::addVote xxx */ INTO `page_vote` (article_id,user_id,`time`) VALUES ('442001','27574631','20180228130846')").columns # ['article_id', 'user_id', 'time'] parser = Parser("SELECT a.* FROM product_a.users AS a JOIN product_b.users AS b ON a.ip_address = b.ip_address") # note that aliases are auto-resolved parser.columns # ['product_a.*', 'product_a.users.ip_address', 'product_b.users.ip_address'] # note that you can also extract columns with their place in the query # which will return dict with lists divided into select, where, order_by, group_by, join, insert and update parser.columns_dict # {'select': ['product_a.users.*'], 'join': ['product_a.users.ip_address', 'product_b.users.ip_address']} ``` ### Extracting columns aliases from query ```python from sql_metadata import Parser parser = Parser("SELECT a, (b + c - u) as alias1, custome_func(d) alias2 from aa, bb order by alias1") # note that columns list do not contain aliases of the columns parser.columns # ["a", "b", "c", "u", "d"] # but you can still extract aliases names parser.columns_aliases_names # ["alias1", "alias2"] # aliases are resolved to the columns which they refer to parser.columns_aliases # {"alias1": ["b", "c", "u"], "alias2": "d"} # you can also extract aliases used by section of the query in which they are used parser.columns_aliases_dict # {"order_by": ["alias1"], "select": ["alias1", "alias2"]} # the same applies to aliases used in queries section when you extract columns_dict # here only the alias is used in order by but it's resolved to actual columns assert parser.columns_dict == {'order_by': ['b', 'c', 'u'], 'select': ['a', 'b', 'c', 'u', 'd']} ``` ### Extracting tables from query ```python from sql_metadata import Parser # get tables from query - for more examples see `tests/test_getting_tables.py` Parser("SELECT a.* FROM product_a.users AS a JOIN product_b.users AS b ON a.ip_address = b.ip_address").tables # ['product_a.users', 'product_b.users'] Parser("SELECT test, id FROM foo, bar").tables # ['foo', 'bar'] # you can also extract aliases of the tables as a dictionary parser = Parser("SELECT f.test FROM foo AS f") # get table aliases parser.tables_aliases # {'f': 'foo'} # note that aliases are auto-resolved for columns parser.columns # ["foo.test"] ``` ### Extracting values from insert query ```python from sql_metadata import Parser parser = Parser( "INSERT /* VoteHelper::addVote xxx */ INTO `page_vote` (article_id,user_id,`time`) " "VALUES ('442001','27574631','20180228130846')" ) # extract values from query parser.values # ["442001", "27574631", "20180228130846"] # extract a dictionary with column-value pairs parser.values_dict #{"article_id": "442001", "user_id": "27574631", "time": "20180228130846"} # if column names are not set auto-add placeholders parser = Parser( "INSERT IGNORE INTO `table` VALUES (9, 2.15, '123', '2017-01-01');" ) parser.values # [9, 2.15, "123", "2017-01-01"] parser.values_dict #{"column_1": 9, "column_2": 2.15, "column_3": "123", "column_4": "2017-01-01"} ``` ### Extracting limit and offset ```python from sql_metadata import Parser Parser('SELECT foo_limit FROM bar_offset LIMIT 50 OFFSET 1000').limit_and_offset # (50, 1000) Parser('SELECT foo_limit FROM bar_offset limit 2000,50').limit_and_offset # (50, 2000) ``` ### Extracting with names ```python from sql_metadata import Parser parser = Parser( """ WITH database1.tableFromWith AS (SELECT aa.* FROM table3 as aa left join table4 on aa.col1=table4.col2), test as (SELECT * from table3) SELECT "xxxxx" FROM database1.tableFromWith alias LEFT JOIN database2.table2 ON ("tt"."ttt"."fff" = "xx"."xxx") """ ) # get names/ aliases of with statements parser.with_names # ["database1.tableFromWith", "test"] # get definition of with queries parser.with_queries # {"database1.tableFromWith": "SELECT aa.* FROM table3 as aa left join table4 on aa.col1=table4.col2" # "test": "SELECT * from table3"} # note that names of with statements do not appear in tables parser.tables # ["table3", "table4", "database2.table2"] ``` ### Extracting sub-queries ```python from sql_metadata import Parser parser = Parser( """ SELECT COUNT(1) FROM (SELECT std.task_id FROM some_task_detail std WHERE std.STATUS = 1) a JOIN (SELECT st.task_id FROM some_task st WHERE task_type_id = 80) b ON a.task_id = b.task_id; """ ) # get sub-queries dictionary parser.subqueries # {"a": "SELECT std.task_id FROM some_task_detail std WHERE std.STATUS = 1", # "b": "SELECT st.task_id FROM some_task st WHERE task_type_id = 80"} # get names/ aliases of sub-queries / derived tables parser.subqueries_names # ["a", "b"] # note that columns coming from sub-queries are resolved to real columns parser.columns #["some_task_detail.task_id", "some_task_detail.STATUS", "some_task.task_id", # "task_type_id"] # same applies for columns_dict, note the join columns are resolved parser.columns_dict #{'join': ['some_task_detail.task_id', 'some_task.task_id'], # 'select': ['some_task_detail.task_id', 'some_task.task_id'], # 'where': ['some_task_detail.STATUS', 'task_type_id']} ``` See `tests` file for more examples of a bit more complex queries. ### Queries normalization and comments extraction ```python from sql_metadata import Parser parser = Parser('SELECT /* Test */ foo FROM bar WHERE id in (1, 2, 56)') # generalize query parser.generalize # 'SELECT foo FROM bar WHERE id in (XYZ)' # remove comments parser.without_comments # 'SELECT foo FROM bar WHERE id in (1, 2, 56)' # extract comments parser.comments # ['/* Test */'] ``` See `test/test_normalization.py` file for more examples of a bit more complex queries. ## Migrating from `sql_metadata` 1.x `sql_metadata.compat` module has been implemented to make the introduction of sql-metadata v2.0 smoother. You can use it by simply changing the imports in your code from: ```python from sql_metadata import get_query_columns, get_query_tables ``` into: ```python from sql_metadata.compat import get_query_columns, get_query_tables ``` The following functions from the old API are available in the `sql_metadata.compat` module: * `generalize_sql` * `get_query_columns` (since #131 columns aliases ARE NOT returned by this function) * `get_query_limit_and_offset` * `get_query_tables` * `get_query_tokens` * `preprocess_query` ## Authors and contributors Created and maintained by [@macbre](https://github.com/macbre) with a great contributions from [@collerek](https://github.com/collerek) and the others. * aborecki (https://github.com/aborecki) * collerek (https://github.com/collerek) * dylanhogg (https://github.com/dylanhogg) * macbre (https://github.com/macbre) ## Stargazers over time [![Stargazers over time](https://starchart.cc/macbre/sql-metadata.svg)](https://starchart.cc/macbre/sql-metadata)

modules.name: sql_metadata.utils
documentation: Module with various utils

functions.name: sql_metadata.utils.flatten_list
documentation: Flattens list of string and lists if there are nested lists.

classes.name: sql_metadata.utils.UniqueList
documentation: List that keeps it's items unique

modules.name: sql_metadata.keywords_lists
documentation:
Module provide lists of sql keywords that should trigger or skip
checks for tables an columns

classes.name: sql_metadata.keywords_lists.QueryType
documentation: Types of supported queries

classes.name: sql_metadata.keywords_lists.TokenType
documentation: Types of SQLTokens

modules.name: sql_metadata
documentation:
Module for parsing sql queries and returning columns,
tables, names of with statements etc.

modules.name: sql_metadata.compat
documentation:
This module provides a temporary compatibility layer
for legacy API dating back to 1.x version.

Change your old imports:

from sql_metadata import get_query_columns, get_query_tables

into:

from sql_metadata.compat import get_query_columns, get_query_tables

modules.name: sql_metadata.token
documentation: Module contains internal SQLToken that creates linked list

classes.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.__str__
documentation: String representation

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.__repr__
documentation: Representation - useful for debugging

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.normalized
documentation: Property returning uppercase value without end lines and spaces

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.stringified_token
documentation:
Returns string representation with whitespace or not - used to rebuild query
from list of tokens

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.last_keyword_normalized
documentation: Property returning uppercase last keyword without end lines and spaces

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_in_parenthesis
documentation: Property checks if token is surrounded with brackets ()

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_create_table_columns_definition
documentation:
Checks if given token is inside columns definition in
create table query like: create table name (<columns def>)

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_keyword_column_name
documentation: Checks if given keyword can be a column name in SELECT query

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_alias_without_as
documentation:
Checks if a given token is an alias without as keyword,
like: SELECT col <alias1>, col2 <alias2> from table

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_alias_definition
documentation:
Returns if current token is a definition of an alias.
Note that aliases can also be used in other queries and be a part
of other nested columns with aliases.

Note that this function only check if alias token is a token with
alias definition, it's not suitable for determining IF token is an alias
as it's more complicated and this method would match
also i.e. sub-queries names

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_alias_of_self
documentation:
Checks if a given token is an alias but at the same time
is also an alias of self, so not really an alias

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_in_with_columns
documentation: Checks if token is inside with colums part of a query

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_wildcard_not_operator
documentation:
Determines if * encountered in query is a wildcard like select <*> from aa
or is that an operator like Select aa <*> bb as cc from dd

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_potential_table_name
documentation: Checks if token is a possible candidate for table name

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_with_statement_nested_in_subquery
documentation: Checks if token is with statement nested in subquery

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_alias_of_table_or_alias_of_subquery
documentation:
Checks if token is alias of table or alias of subquery

It's not a list of tables, e.g. SELECT * FROM foo, bar
hence, it can be the case of alias without AS, e.g. SELECT * FROM foo bar
or an alias of subquery (SELECT * FROM foo) bar

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_a_wildcard_in_select_statement
documentation:
Checks if token is a wildcard in select statement

Handle * wildcard in select part, but ignore count(*)

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_potential_column_name
documentation: Checks if token is a potential column name

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_conversion_specifier
documentation: Checks if token is a format or data type in cast or convert

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_column_name_inside_insert_clause
documentation:
Checks if token is a column name inside insert clause,
e.g. INSERT INTO `foo` (col1, `col2`) VALUES (..)

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_potential_alias
documentation: Checks if given token can possibly be an alias

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_a_valid_alias
documentation: Checks if given token meets the alias criteria

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.next_token_not_comment
documentation: Property returning next non-comment token

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.previous_token_not_comment
documentation: Property returning previous non-comment token

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_constraint_definition_inside_create_table_clause
documentation:
Checks if token is constraint definition inside create table clause

Used to handle CREATE TABLE queries (#35) to skip keyword that are withing
parenthesis-wrapped list of column

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_columns_alias_of_with_query_or_column_in_insert_query
documentation:
Check if token is column alias of with query or column in insert query

We are in <columns> of INSERT INTO <TABLE> (<columns>),
or columns of with statement: with (<columns>) as ...

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_sub_query_alias
documentation: Checks for aliases of sub-queries i.e.: SELECT from (...) <alias>

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_with_query_name
documentation: checks for names of the with queries <name> as (subquery)

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_sub_query_name_or_with_name_or_function_name
documentation: Check for non applicable names: with, subquery or custom function

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_not_an_alias_or_is_self_alias_outside_of_subquery
documentation: Checks if token is not alias or alias of self outside of sub query

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_table_definition_suffix_in_non_select_create_table
sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_column_definition_inside_create_table
documentation:
Checks for column names in create table

Previous token is either ( or , -> indicates the column name

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.is_potential_column_alias
documentation: Checks if column can be an alias

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.token_is_alias_of_self_not_from_subquery
documentation:
Checks if token is also an alias, but is an alias of self that is not
coming from a subquery, that means it's a valid column

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.token_name_is_same_as_alias_not_from_subquery
documentation:
Checks if token is also an alias, but is an alias of self that is not
coming from a subquery, that means it's a valid column

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.table_prefixed_column
documentation: Substitutes table alias with actual table name

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.get_nth_previous
documentation: Function iterates previous tokens getting nth previous token

methods.name: sql_metadata.token.SQLToken.find_nearest_token
documentation:
Returns token with given value to the left or right.
If value is not found it returns EmptyToken.
Class representing single token and connected into linked list

modules.name: sql_metadata.generalizator
documentation: Module used to produce generalized sql out of given query

classes.name: sql_metadata.generalizator.Generalizator

methods.name: sql_metadata.generalizator.Generalizator._normalize_likes
documentation:
Normalize and wrap LIKE statements

\:type sql str
\:rtype: str
SQL queries normalization (#16)

methods.name: sql_metadata.generalizator.Generalizator.without_comments
documentation:
Removes comments from SQL query

```
rtype

str
```

methods.name: sql_metadata.generalizator.Generalizator.generalize
documentation:
Removes most variables from an SQL query
and replaces them with X or N for numbers.

Based on Mediawiki's DatabaseBase::generalizeSQL
Class used to produce generalized sql out of given query

modules.name: sql_metadata.parser
documentation: This module provides SQL query parsing functions
pylint: disable=C0302

classes.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.query
documentation: Returns preprocessed query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.query_type
documentation:
Returns type of the query.
Currently supported queries are:
select, insert, update, replace, create table, alter table, with + select

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.tokens
documentation: Tokenizes the query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.columns
documentation: Returns the list columns this query refers to

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.columns_dict
documentation:
Returns dictionary of column names divided into section of the query in which
given column is present.

Sections consist of: select, where, order_by, group_by, join, insert and update

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.columns_aliases
documentation: Returns a dictionary of column aliases with columns

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.columns_aliases_dict
documentation:
Returns dictionary of column names divided into section of the query in which
given column is present.

Sections consist of: select, where, order_by, group_by, join, insert and update

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.columns_aliases_names
documentation: Extract names of the column aliases used in query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.tables
documentation: Return the list of tables this query refers to

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.limit_and_offset
documentation: Returns value for limit and offset if set

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.tables_aliases
sql_metadata.parser.Parser.with_names
documentation:
Returns with statements aliases list from a given query

E.g. WITH database1.tableFromWith AS (SELECT * FROM table3)
     SELECT "xxxxx" FROM database1.tableFromWith alias
     LEFT JOIN database2.table2 ON ("tt"."ttt"."fff" = "xx"."xxx")
will return ["database1.tableFromWith"]

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.with_queries
documentation:
Returns "WITH" subqueries with names

E.g. WITH tableFromWith AS (SELECT * FROM table3)
     SELECT "xxxxx" FROM database1.tableFromWith alias
     LEFT JOIN database2.table2 ON ("tt"."ttt"."fff" = "xx"."xxx")
will return {"tableFromWith": "SELECT * FROM table3"}

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.subqueries
documentation: Returns a dictionary with all sub-queries existing in query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.subqueries_names
documentation:
Returns sub-queries aliases list from a given query

e.g. SELECT COUNT(1) FROM
    (SELECT std.task_id FROM some_task_detail std WHERE std.STATUS = 1) a
     JOIN (SELECT st.task_id FROM some_task st WHERE task_type_id = 80) b
     ON a.task_id = b.task_id;
will return ["a", "b"]

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.values
documentation: Returns list of values from insert queries

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.values_dict
documentation:
Returns dictionary of column-value pairs.
If columns are not set the auto generated column_<col_number> are added.

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.comments
documentation: Return comments from SQL query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.without_comments
documentation: Removes comments from SQL query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser.generalize
documentation:
Removes most variables from an SQL query
and replaces them with X or N for numbers.

Based on Mediawiki's DatabaseBase::generalizeSQL

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._not_parsed_tokens
documentation: Returns only tokens that have no type assigned yet

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._add_to_columns_subsection
documentation: Add columns to the section in which it appears in query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._add_to_columns_aliases_subsection
documentation: Add alias to the section in which it appears in query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._resolve_column_alias
documentation: Returns a column name for a given alias

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._resolve_alias_to_column
documentation: Resolves aliases of tables to already resolved columns

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._resolve_sub_queries
documentation:
Resolve column names coming from sub queries and with queries to actual
column names as they appear in the query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._resolve_nested_query
documentation: Resolves subquery reference to the actual column in the subquery

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._is_with_query_already_resolved
documentation:
Checks if columns comes from a with query that has columns defined
cause if it does that means that column name is an alias and is already
resolved in aliases.

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._determine_opening_parenthesis_type
documentation: Determines the type of left parenthesis in query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._determine_closing_parenthesis_type
documentation: Determines the type of right parenthesis in query

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._find_all_columns_between_tokens
documentation: Returns a list of columns between two tokens

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._preprocess_query
documentation: Perform initial query cleanup

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._is_token_part_of_complex_identifier
documentation:
Checks if token is a part of complex identifier like
<schema>.<table>.<column> or <table/sub_query>.<column>

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._combine_qualified_names
documentation: Combines names like <schema>.<table>.<column> or <table/sub_query>.<column>

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._combine_tokens
documentation: Checks if complex identifier is longer and follows back until it's finished

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._get_sqlparse_tokens
documentation: Flattens the tokens and removes whitespace

methods.name: sql_metadata.parser.Parser._get_switch_by_create_query
documentation: Return the switch that creates query type.
Main class to parse sql query
